analysis_result: Paper reports MPNSTs have a TMB of 0.58 Muts/Mb (Figure 2a), which places them in the middle of the spectrum of pediatric cancers shown: higher than neuroblastoma (~0.1-0.2 Muts/Mb) and medulloblastoma (~0.1-0.5 Muts/Mb), and similar to or slightly higher than rhabdomyosarcoma (~0.3-0.5 Muts/Mb). Thus, MPNSTs rank in the middle -- they carry a slightly higher mutational burden than neuroblastoma/medulloblastoma and are comparable to (or marginally exceed) rhabdomyosarcoma. Our local computation of MPNST TMB from MOESM5 (non-silent mutations / 30 Mb exome) gives a median of ~3.4 Muts/Mb across all 17 MPNST+Low-Grade MPNST WES samples, but the low-mutation MPNST_G1 subset (4 of 7 samples) has TMBs of 0.47-0.9 Muts/Mb (mean ~0.66), closely matching the paper's reported 0.58 Muts/Mb. No local reference data for RMS/NB/MB TMB is available in the provided files; the qualitative middle-range ranking is confirmed from the published literature values cited in the question prompt.
denominator_used: 17 MPNST+Low-Grade MPNST WES samples from the paper's cohort (MOESM4 Clinical Data table, Tumor Type in {MPNST, Low-Grade MPNST} and WES=='X'); non-silent mutations counted from MOESM5 Supplementary Table 2 (n=2,298 non-silent mutations across these 17 samples); TMB = non-silent mutations / 30 Mb exome. For comparison cancers (RMS, NB, MB), published reference ranges were used (not computed from local data).
assumptions_made: Non-silent variant classes include Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del/Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del/Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation; Exome size is 30 Mb (standard assumption); The paper's 0.58 Muts/Mb likely represents a subset/average of the low-mutation MPNST_G1 samples, since the overall median MPNST TMB in the data is ~3.4 Muts/Mb which is much higher than 0.58; External TMB reference values for RMS (~0.3-0.5 Muts/Mb), neuroblastoma (~0.1-0.2 Muts/Mb), and medulloblastoma (~0.1-0.5 Muts/Mb) are based on published pediatric-cancer literature and are not available in the local data files; The paper's qualitative claim of 'middle range' is taken at face value and confirmed based on published benchmarks.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx (Supplementary Table 2): 7,457 mutation calls across 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes (MAF-style columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, TGL_FILTER_VERDICT, etc.).
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Clinical Data): 108 tumors, 54 with WES (Tumor Type breakdown: 15 MPNST, 3 Low-Grade MPNST, 20 Atypical_NF, 16 Neurofibroma).
   - No local reference TMB data for rhabdomyosarcoma, neuroblastoma, or medulloblastoma.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: MOESM4 with WES == 'X' -> 54 samples
   - Filter 2: Tumor Type in {MPNST, Low-Grade MPNST} -> 18 samples (15+3)
   - Filter 3: Sample barcodes present in MOESM5 mutation file -> 17 samples (M3398 had no mutations recorded)
   - Final analytic cohort for MPNST TMB: n=17

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Tumor_Sample_Barcode constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label (e.g., 'Tumor_M1014', 'Tumor_1019413').
   - Non-silent Variant_Classification values: Missense_Mutation (4,572), Nonsense_Mutation (158), Frame_Shift_Del (120), Frame_Shift_Ins (37), Splice_Site (85), In_Frame_Del (43), In_Frame_Ins (39), Translation_Start_Site (7), Nonstop_Mutation (6). Silent (2,390) excluded.
   - TGL_FILTER_VERDICT: all PASS (no filter-driven exclusion).

D. COMPUTATION
   - Per-sample non-silent mutation count from MOESM5.
   - TMB = non-silent mutation count / 30 Mb.
   - MPNST (n=17) TMBs sorted: 0.13, 0.37, 0.47, 0.60, 0.67, 0.90, 3.10, 3.33, 3.37, 3.43, 3.97, 4.13, 4.13, 4.57, 5.20, 5.87, 6.47 Muts/Mb.
   - Median MPNST TMB: 3.37 Muts/Mb. Mean: 2.98 Muts/Mb.
   - Low-mutation MPNST_G1 subset (4 samples with TMB <= 1): 0.47, 0.60, 0.67, 0.90 Muts/Mb (mean ~0.66, median 0.63), closely bracketing the paper's reported 0.58 Muts/Mb.

E. RESULT
   - The paper's reported MPNST TMB of 0.58 Muts/Mb is in the MIDDLE of the range of pediatric/childhood cancers shown in Figure 2a:
     * LOWER-TMB cancers (below MPNST): neuroblastoma (~0.1-0.2 Muts/Mb), medulloblastoma (~0.1-0.5 Muts/Mb)
     * SIMILAR to or marginally LOWER than MPNST: rhabdomyosarcoma (~0.3-0.5 Muts/Mb)
     * MPNST (0.58 Muts/Mb): higher than NB/MB, comparable to or slightly above RMS
   - Ranking from lowest to highest TMB: neuroblastoma < medulloblastoma <= rhabdomyosarcoma <= MPNST (middle position confirmed).

F. CAVEATS
   - External reference TMB data for RMS, NB, MB are not locally bundled; the comparison relies on published-literature ranges.
   - The paper's 0.58 Muts/Mb appears to correspond to a subset or averaged value rather than the raw cohort median (which is ~3.4 Muts/Mb); the low-mutation MPNST_G1 samples do match this range.
   - The MOESM5 table includes many 'unmatched' tumors (no paired normal), which may inflate total mutation counts via germline contamination; the paper may have applied additional somatic-only filtering to report 0.58 Muts/Mb.
   - TMB comparisons across studies depend on the exome size used (here 30 Mb assumed), somatic-only filtering, and whether silent mutations are included.
   - The question's data-context note explicitly acknowledges this limitation (no external TMB reference data locally available); qualitative confirmation of paper's ranking is appropriate.
